name: Smoky Tarragon Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the lemon and fruitwood chips with salt and pepper.
2. Cut chickens into quarters. Remove the backbone, flatten the breasts using a cleaver or your palm, remove the last joint from wings (wingtips), and cut slits in the chicken legs.
3. Squeeze the juice from the lemon. Mix together the parsley, tarragon, lemon juice, olive oil, and seasonings to create a marinade.
4. Rub the marinade into the chicken pieces and cover. Marinate for several hours ahead if preparing early.
5. Start the grill fire. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
6. Place dark meat pieces about 6 inches from coals and cook, turning every 5 minutes, until fully cooked (approximately 25 minutes total).
7. After cooking dark meat for about 12 minutes, add soaked fruitwood chips to the coals. Add chicken breast pieces, cover grill, let smoke permeate meat for at least 5 minutes.
8. Remove grill cover and continue cooking until internal temperature reaches safe level (about 7 more minutes). No basting needed as skin has enough fat to self-baste.
Yield: 4 servings.
